Alvelal

network_affiliation
[[Commonland]]
background_section
## Background The Altiplano Estepario spans five districts at the intersection of Almería, Murcia and Granada, three provinces belonging to Spain's southernmost region, Andalucía. This landscape is a semi-arid steppe—a large area of flat unforested grassland—that forms a transition zone between desert and Mediterranean forest. The bioregion encompasses [over 1 million hectares across more than 80 municipalities](https://youtu.be/QL35HCi30Ac), creating a vast territory where water scarcity has shaped human settlement patterns for centuries. Despite these challenging conditions, the economy today is mainly based on agriculture, with traditional farming and pastoral activities playing important cultural and economic roles. Yet the landscape tells a story of both loss and potential. Mediterranean forests once covered vast areas of the territory—trees like oaks and evergreen shrubs such as thyme and rosemary. However, [desertification and extreme climate conditions](https://commonland.com/landscapes/reversing-desertification-with-regenerative-practices/), combined with unsustainable land management practices, have reduced local biodiversity and climate resilience. This leads to both social and economic losses as young people leave and entrepreneurship becomes increasingly difficult. ### Origins and Network Since 2014, Commonland's landscape partner AlVelAl has been using the 4 Returns Framework to bring together local farmers, conservationists, government, and entrepreneurs to create a common vision for the territory. The alliance is made up of [several entities](https://youtu.be/QL35HCi30Ac) that have a common vision of achieving the regeneration of this territory. ![[Plan-AlVelAl-2024-5 (arrastrado).jpg]] As one director reflects: "At the moment people move away from the region to find work. I'm inspired by being part of a project that transforms a territory for the younger generation, so that my daughters can choose to live here."
Sources

place
Altiplano Estepario, Andalucía, Spain
activities

temp
issues with location
description
A collaborative alliance operating across over 1 million hectares in southeastern Spain, using Commonland's Four Returns Framework to reverse desertification and restore prosperity. Since 2014, AlVelAl Association has grown to more than 490 members including 280 farmers, developing regenerative businesses and coordinating landscape-scale restoration through the Altiplano Regeneration Manifesto, community machinery banks, and regenerative education programs that merge art, biodiversity, and emotional connection to land.
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy The vision by 2034 is clear: the AlVelAl Territory becomes a regenerative landscape where nature and agriculture are in balance. An ecological corridor connects the region's mountain ranges. Farms with both natural and productive lands form a mosaic of biodiversity hotspots, which act as stepping zones between natural areas. Rural abandonment is reversed as young people return and find new opportunities. The area continues to regenerate, led by a group of diverse, inspired local stakeholders. Current progress includes: [71 initiatives](https://commonland.com/landscapes/reversing-desertification-with-regenerative-practices/) including art projects, demo pilots, and farms, available to raise awareness for local communities; [216 farmers](https://commonland.com/landscapes/reversing-desertification-with-regenerative-practices/) implementing improved management practices in line with regenerative agriculture; [14,509 hectares](https://commonland.com/landscapes/reversing-desertification-with-regenerative-practices/) under direct improved management and restoration in combined and natural zones; and [43 locally run businesses](https://commonland.com/landscapes/reversing-desertification-with-regenerative-practices/) benefiting from increased access to new markets and value chain infrastructure. The Altiplano Estepario continues to serve as a lighthouse example of holistic landscape restoration based on the 4 Returns Framework in drylands areas, inspiring other initiatives across Spain and Portugal as they work toward creating a 4 Returns Network across the Iberian Peninsula. This approach recognizes that ecological restoration must work alongside economic activities, creating a mosaic where natural areas connect through productive landscapes managed regeneratively.
website
https://alvelal.es/
bioregional_context
The Altiplano Estepario spans five districts at the intersection of Almería, Murcia and Granada, three provinces belonging to Spain's southernmost region, Andalucía. It is a semi-arid steppe and transition zone between desert and Mediterranean forest. The bioregion encompasses over 1 million hectares across more than 80 municipalities. Water has always been scarce in this landscape, yet the area has been home to human settlements for centuries. Desertification and extreme climate conditions, combined with unsustainable land management practices, have reduced local biodiversity and climate resilience, making this a challenging landscape for local communities.
category
